What specific characteristic do waterfalls and the hanging sweater share that makes this comparison effective?

questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

responses.content: in the part where he says “Jiang Ji-li has always done well at school. In addition to doing very well in her studies" I could see he liked her as a student in general
because thin face was there and maybe thin face purposely wanted  to expose her
I could see when thin face said that “That’s very good. We knew that you had more sense than to follow your father,” that he possibly wanted something from her

However, it misses the main themes of fear, loss of education, and oppression under Taliban rule. The answer is vague and does not fully capture the emotional or experiential impact described in the text, but it does not contradict the text or provide a generic answer. Therefore, it fits best in the 'partial-understanding' category.
